citations.description: The absence-refuted veto is per-finding, not intra-verdict cross-checking.
partition_findings assembles the verdict but contains no intra-verdict contradiction check.

findings.criterion: Validation assessment cross-checks findings within a single verdict for mutual contradiction and drops the weaker/contradicted one; a fixture reproduces the A1 blocking-vs-advisory contradiction and asserts at most one survives.
findings.detail: No implementation of intra-verdict cross-checking was found. The absence-refuted veto (veto:absence-refuted in decide.py) handles the A1 false-absence scenario but works on a per-finding basis without reference to sibling findings in the same verdict. No code path in orchestrator.py, passes.py, workflow_ops.py, or review_kernel/decide.py compares findings against each other within a single verdict to detect mutual contradiction and drop the weaker one. No test fixture reproducing the specific A1 blocking-vs-advisory contradiction scenario (one verdict asserting 'no one is tasked with capture' blocking while simultaneously carrying 'the parent explicitly assigns capture to S1' advisory) was found.

citations.description: Exhaustive search across src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ for 'comment trail', 'comment_trail', 'litigated', 'conceded', and 'resolved' returned no matches in the implementation.

findings.criterion: Validation assessment consults the ticket's recorded comment trail and drops a finding that re-litigates a point the trail already resolved; a fixture reproduces B3 (rebase:chain conceded in-trail) and asserts it is dropped.
findings.detail: No evidence found of comment trail consultation anywhere in the plan review pipeline. Searched all plan_review module files (passes.py, orchestrator.py, workflow_ops.py, det_floor.py, __init__.py, attest.py) and the review_kernel for any mechanism that reads ticket comments and uses them to drop findings already resolved in the trail. No fixture reproducing the B3 scenario was found.

citations.description: test_backstop_never_touches_llm_tier_findings confirms LLM-tier subject-less findings are NOT dropped by the backstop.

findings.criterion: Finding discovery de-duplicates findings and suppresses contentless findings (empty body / no checklist_item); a fixture reproduces B2/B4.
findings.detail: DET-tier contentless finding suppression is implemented (det_finding_has_subject in det_floor.py) and tested (test_a8e5_det_hygiene_edge.py). However, B2 ('Acceptance-criteria/definition-of-done quality issues' — empty body, no checklist_item) and B4 ('rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader trailer-preservation unverified' — duplicate findings A11 and A13) were LLM-tier findings. The test test_backstop_never_touches_llm_tier_findings explicitly confirms that subject-less LLM-tier findings are NOT dropped by the hygiene backstop. No LLM-tier deduplication or contentless-suppression mechanism was found in orchestrator.py or passes.py. No fixture specifically reproducing B2/B4 was found.

citations.description: ADR 0008 covers novelty floor for plan-edit convergence but does not record the remediation cascade anti-pattern.
Search for 'remediation cascade', 'advisory must not manufacture a block', and 're-review-stability' across all docs/ and src/ returned no matches.

findings.criterion: A short design note (or ADR) records the 'remediation cascade' anti-pattern (acting on an advisory must not manufacture a block) and the re-review-stability contract.
findings.detail: ADR 0008 (docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md) covers the novelty-floor remediation convergence but does not record the 'remediation cascade' anti-pattern ('acting on an advisory must not manufacture a block'). The drift_floor.py module docstring and plan-review-gate.md document the code-drift mechanism but neither records the named anti-pattern or the re-review-stability contract as specified. Exhaustive search for 'remediation cascade', 'advisory must not manufacture', 're-review-stability contract', and 'acting on an advisory' across the entire docs/ tree returned no matches.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
